rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE BENJAMIN YEE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/13/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) On Schedule C please explain entry 06/10/2021 Jennifer Croteau 3/2-4/27 for $325.00. 2) On Schedule C under legal costs please file the invoices for all entries except for the 3/18/2020 entry which is based on the 03/16/2020 order. 3) On Schedule C please explain why $322.34 was disbursed to Jennifer Croteau for mileage, tolls, parking when LRSF 14.57 says mileage, parking, cab/rideshare fares, and tolls are absorbed as part of fees. It appears either this needs to be paid back to the trust or deducted from the trustee's fee request. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 3/28/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JULIA CASEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/13/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59.E.] Notwithstanding Administrator's allegation that there is "no set requirement" in the Probate Code, a PETITION accompanying the report on the status of estate administration is implied when an estate has not closed within the statutory time for closing an estate pursuant to Probate Code Section 12200, for which report/petition NOTICE is required to be given, pursuant to Probate Code Section 12201(b). The only proper form of NOTICE for decedents' estates (other than Probate Code Section 850 petitions) is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120. Courtesy copies of the âStatus Reportâ and the proof of service and a proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 4/4/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MIKE ANGEL LEON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Appearance required. Counsel should be prepared to address the following issues: 1. How does the San Francisco Eagle Bar, LLC (the "LLC"), have standing to oppose this motion to preliminarily enjoin an individual? 2. Does the Administrator intend to file an action under Probate Code Section 850 or another vehicle to marshal the LLC as an asset of the estate? 3. Why were the business records of the LLC moved to an off-site storage facility? 4. When were the business records of the LLC moved to an off-site storage facility? 5. Why should the court consider pleadings that do not comply with the email requirement of CRC 3.2.111? (See CCP Section 1010.6(e)(1) which requires a party represented by counsel to accept electronic service of a notice or document that may be served by mail, express mail, overnight delivery, or facsimile transmission.)

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF EDUARDO A. MARROQUIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/30/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 3/14/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FLORENCE TRAUTMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/4/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sale must be filed to support the statutory fee. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 7.651, the petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. [LRSF 14.35 I.6.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues (presumably a supplement to the petition, which must be prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the executor) must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/23/22. (The executor of the will was appointed on 4/6/21. The order appointing him was not filed, however, until 4/8/21.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE ONG FAMILY TRUST DATED MAY 28, 2002
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition For: 1) Order For Accounting; 2) Order For Damages, Attorney'S Fees And Costs For Failure To Timely Provide Notice Of Trust Under Pc
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/13/2022. 1) The proof of service shows notice was given on 11/29/2021, less than 30 days before the 12/22/2021 hearing. New notice of the continued hearing must be timely given. 2) Notice must be given on a mandatory Judicial Council form DE-115 with respect to the relief under Probate Code sections 850, 856, and 859 sought by the petition. The trustee must be served in the manner required by section 851(a). Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PATRICIA SMITH WOOD TRUST DATED SEPTEMBER 28, 1992, as amended
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Proabet 850 Petition For 1. Order To Reform Conveyance Documents; And 2. Order Confirmeing Trust Ownership Of And Conveyance Of Title To Bank
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required for counsel to sddress the nature of this pleading, as it appears to be a quiet title action in that it seeks to correct 30 years of deeds and recorded trust documents, including legal description, name change, and closing breaks in the chain of title. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDITH C. BASTIANI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO DETERMINE SUCCESSION TO REAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/04/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) The Supplemental petition must be filed as an amended petition and re-noticed. Matter continued to allow time for proper notice. 2) Please explain the legal basis / cite probate code sections for this petition being used for unclaimed property held by the state controller. 3) Please file the deed for the real property. 4) Please file copies of the Wills mentioned in the petition. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 3/21/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAKO U ROVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/11/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 41.59 E.] No proof of publication. The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. If a will is lost, the petition and notice must clearly state that the petitioner seeks to admit a lost will. [LRSF 14.20.] If the petitioner seeks to be appointed as the administrator with [lost] will annexed, then he should not be seeking appointment as just administrator. If a person entitled to notice cannot be located, the petitioner must file a declaration describing efforts made to locate the person and file an ex parte petition to dispense with notice to that person. [CRC 7.52.] If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. (There might be additional issues with the petition; it was only cursorily skimmed online.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/30/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELIZABETH A. LEAF
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
